title: Campylocentrum fasciola
intro: Campylocentrum fasciola is a species of epiphytic orchid native to Central and South America, characterized by its unique, strap-like leaves and delicate white flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate respiratory issues and boost the immune system. Bioactive constituents of Campylocentrum fasciola include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Campylocentrum fasciola is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in the production of essential oils and capsules.
taxonomy: Campylocentrum fasciola is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Campylocentrum, which is a group of orchids that are native to Central and South America. The genus Campylocentrum is part of the subfamily Epidendroideae, which is a large and diverse group of orchids. This subfamily is part of the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. The classification of Campylocentrum fasciola reflects its evolutionary relationships with other orchid species.
